metadata.title: Implement Background Sync and Cache Management

story.iWant: the app to keep my Google Calendar cache up to date in the background
story.soThat: I always have current data without needing to trigger manual sync repeatedly
story.tasks: - Verify Stories 2.2-2.4 prerequisites and reuse their sync/status contracts instead of creating a second sync stack - Add a machine-readable failure reason so expired Google sync tokens do not require brittle string parsing - Implement GoogleCalendarService.FetchIncrementalEventsAsync with sync-token pagination and 410 detection - Extend SyncManager for background incremental sync, missing-token fallback, and full-resync recovery - Add INetworkMonitor/NetworkMonitor and BackgroundSyncService using PeriodicTimer plus NetworkChange - Seed gcal_background_sync_interval_minutes and start the background service from App.OnLaunched - Publish a SyncCompletedMessage for Story 2.4 status-refresh consumers - Add unit and integration coverage for timer, offline, no-overlap, and 410 fallback behavior

acceptanceCriteria.ac: When the app is running and online, a configurable 30-minute background timer triggers incremental sync.
When the timer fires offline, the sync is skipped silently and retried later without showing an error.
When Google returns HTTP 410 for an expired sync token, SyncManager clears the token and falls back to a full re-sync.
After background sync completes, the UI sync-status surface refreshes and data_source_refresh stores the new token and timestamp.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Defines the canonical background flow: timer fire, network check, SyncManager incremental path, token lookup in data_source_refresh, 410 fallback to full sync, then metadata update.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Authoritative acceptance criteria limit Story 2.5 to configurable timer-driven incremental sync, silent offline skip, 410 fallback, and status/refresh updates after completion.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Offline sync attempts log and retry later; Polly retries transient failures; expired sync tokens produce a deterministic full-resync recovery path.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Provides the business intent for background sync and notes offline-capable behavior, while the tech spec supplies the narrower implementation contract.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: Confirms the local-first cache strategy and that Google Calendar change tracking is based on incremental sync tokens stored in refresh metadata.

doc.title: Database Schemas
doc.snippet: Confirms that config values are persisted in the config table, gcal_event is the local cache, and data_source_refresh is the sync bookkeeping table.

doc.title: Story 1.6
doc.snippet: Reinforces current logging/DI rules: constructor-injected ILogger, services.AddLogging(builder => builder.AddSerilog()), and friendly user-facing errors.

file.reason: The app uses plain ServiceCollection, not the generic host. Background sync must be registered and started manually from startup code.
Already contains the FetchIncrementalEventsAsync signature that Story 2.5 must finally implement.
Auth flow already exists here. Both fetch methods are still NotImplemented and the existing CreateUserCredential path should be reused for incremental sync.
Currently an empty placeholder record. Story 2.5 depends on the real DTO introduced by Story 2.2.
Must already include SyncToken support from Story 2.2 so background incremental sync can persist and rotate the Google token.
Current seeded settings live here; add gcal_background_sync_interval_minutes rather than inventing a new settings store.
Shows the current WeakReferenceMessenger and existing Google-settings surface that background sync should complement, not replace.
Demonstrates the current message-pattern footprint; Story 2.5 should add SyncCompletedMessage in the same folder and style.
Confirms xUnit/Moq/FluentAssertions and the x64 project-reference pattern required for new timer and sync tests.

package.name: System.Net.NetworkInformation.NetworkChange
package.note: Use for network-availability notifications via the BCL.

package.name: System.Threading.PeriodicTimer
package.note: Use for the background sync loop without blocking the UI thread.

constraints.constraint: Keep implementation in the existing root-level Services/Data/ViewModels/Messages layout. Do not introduce a new Core project or generic-host stack.
Story 2.5 depends on Story 2.2 sync plumbing and Story 2.3 update/delete handling. Reuse those paths instead of duplicating them.
Story 2.4 owns sync-status indicator rendering. Story 2.5 should publish refresh notifications and integrate with that surface, not redesign the UI.
Do not use string matching on ErrorMessage to detect expired tokens. Add a structured failure reason for sync-token expiry.
Follow the tech spec's sync-token incremental design. Do not pivot to a separate updatedMin-only design for background sync.
Persist the timer interval in the existing config table under gcal_background_sync_interval_minutes. Do not add a new settings table or JSON file.
Background work must be started manually from App.OnLaunched and disposed on app shutdown. It must never block the UI thread.
Prevent overlapping manual and background sync runs with a single-flight guard such as SemaphoreSlim.
Use ILogger<T> and audit-log patterns already established in the repo. Offline skips and 410 fallback should be logged, but routine background events must not surface modal dialogs.
The high-level epic text mentions cache-size warnings, but the authoritative Story 2.5 tech spec does not. Do not add speculative database-size monitoring in this story.

tests.standards: xUnit + Moq + FluentAssertions. Prefer unit tests for timer, network, and 410 branching; use SQLite-backed integration tests for refresh-metadata persistence and seeded config verification.

ideas.test: Fake one timer tick with network available and assert one incremental sync invocation.
Return offline from INetworkMonitor and assert no sync call, no exception, and no UI interaction.
Simulate an offline skip followed by NetworkAvailabilityChanged(true) and assert one catch-up run occurs.
Hold SyncManager in-flight and assert a second timer fire is skipped.
Mock multi-page delta responses and assert all items plus the final replacement sync token are returned.
Mock Google 410 and assert the returned failure reason is sync-token-expired.
No stored token should route to the existing full-sync path exactly once.
Simulate sync-token-expired and assert token cleared, full sync rerun, and replacement token persisted.
Run a successful background-triggered sync against SQLite-backed persistence and assert refresh metadata plus a sync-completed notification.
Verify the config table contains gcal_background_sync_interval_minutes with default value 30 after migration.
